title: Rich Korean Tofu Pilaf
description: A rich and crispy Korean dish featuring lemongrass stalks, knead, suitable for halal dietary needs.

ingredients.name: 400g firm tofu
ingredients.notes: Ensure it's fully drained and pressed to achieve the desired texture.

ingredients.name: 1 cup short-grain rice
ingredients.notes: Rinse thoroughly to remove excess starch.

ingredients.name: 1 can (400ml) chicken broth
ingredients.notes: Can be replaced with water for a vegetarian version.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons mirin or rice vinegar
ingredients.notes: Mirin is preferred for authentic taste.

ingredients.name: 5 lemongrass stalks, cut into 3 pieces each
ingredients.notes: Use the white part of the stalks only.

instructions: Prepare lemongrass by pounding it lightly between your palms until fragrant, then mince finely.
Heat sesame oil in a wok or large skillet over medium heat. Add onion and garlic; sauté for about 2 minutes until softened.
Add tofu pieces to the pan; stir-fry for another minute to develop color and texture.
Pour in chicken broth, soy sauce, mirin (or rice vinegar), and honey/maple syrup if using. Bring to a simmer.
Stir in lemongrass, cover with a lid, and let it cook for 2 minutes without disturbing the mixture.
Gently fold in rinsed short-grain rice; distribute evenly across the pan so that it doesn't stick or clump up.
Return the lid and continue cooking for about 10 more minutes until liquid is absorbed and rice grains are tender but not mushy.
Stir a final time to mix everything together, then remove from heat. Serve immediately with sliced green onions on top as a garnish.
